headline: The Great Rotation Is Here. Why Money Is Fleeing Chips for Old Favorites.
summary: U.S. stocks remain deep in the throes of a major rotation heading into the teeth of the second-quarter earnings season, and the traditional August lull that follows, as investors take money from highflying tech sector and spread that cash across a host of old-economy bets.  What’s happening alongside that “great rotation,” however, is also interesting, and likely keeping stocks afloat amid the broader tech declines.

headline: The Trade Desk Appoints Vinny Rinaldi as Vice President of Client Strategy & Growth
summary: VENTURA, Calif., July 16, 2026--The Trade Desk, a leading advertising technology company, today announced that Vinny Rinaldi has joined the company as Vice President of Client Strategy & Growth. In this role, Rinaldi will partner closely with marketers to help them unlock greater value from data-driven advertising, navigate the rapidly evolving media landscape and accelerate business growth through the premium open internet. Rinaldi will report to Chief Operating Officer, Vivek Kundra.

summary: U.S. stocks remain deep in the throes of a major rotation heading into the teeth of the second-quarter earnings season, and the traditional August lull that follows, as investors take money from highflying tech sector and spread that cash across a host of old-economy bets. headline: US probes Samsung for alleged infringement of Netlist's memory-chip patents
summary: U.S. trade regulators have launched a probe into Samsung Electronics' memory chips and products sold by Google, ‌Nvidia, Broadcom and Super Micro Computer that use them following ‌a complaint by Netlist alleging infringement of its patents.  California-based Netlist has accused Samsung and ​its U.S. units of infringing its patents on dynamic random access memory, a type of chip that temporarily stores data for processors and is a critical component in the servers powering the AI boom, the U.S. ‌International Trade Commission said ⁠on Wednesday.  Netlist has asked the USITC to block imports of the disputed chips and products and order the ⁠companies to stop selling them in the U.S. An ITC judge will hold an evidentiary hearing and issue an initial ruling, subject to review ​by the ​commission.

raw.headline: US probes Samsung for alleged infringement of Netlist's memory-chip patents
raw.summary: U.S. trade regulators have launched a probe into Samsung Electronics' memory chips and products sold by Google, ‌Nvidia, Broadcom and Super Micro Computer that use them following ‌a complaint by Netlist alleging infringement of its patents.  California-based Netlist has accused Samsung and ​its U.S. units of infringing its patents on dynamic random access memory, a type of chip that temporarily stores data for processors and is a critical component in the servers powering the AI boom, the U.S. ‌International Trade Commission said ⁠on Wednesday.  Netlist has asked the USITC to block imports of the disputed chips and products and order the ⁠companies to stop selling them in the U.S. An ITC judge will hold an evidentiary hearing and issue an initial ruling, subject to review ​by the ​commission.

headline: Google loses court fight against $854,250 Italian fine over gambling advertising
summary: Alphabet unit Google on Thursday lost its fight against a €750,000 ($854,250) fine imposed for gambling ‌advertising on its YouTube video platform four years ago as ‌Europe's top court sided with Italy's communication authority.  Google had challenged the fine handed out by ​an Italian administrative court in 2022, prompting that court to seek guidance from the Luxembourg-based Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU).  ⁠The YouTube videos promoting online gambling had been uploaded by a content creator with a commercial partnership deal with ⁠Google.

raw.headline: Google loses court fight against $854,250 Italian fine over gambling advertising

Google had challenged the fine handed out by ​an Italian administrative court in 2022, prompting that court to seek guidance from the Luxembourg-based Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU).  ⁠The YouTube videos promoting online gambling had been uploaded by a content creator with a commercial partnership deal with ⁠Google.
